description:
DSRs uses string split for parsing, DSPy uses regex which is more robust.

**Problem:**
DSRs (chat.rs:593-594):
  .split(format!('[[ ## {field_name} ## ]]\n').as_str())
  String split can fail on edge cases

DSPy (chat_adapter.py:21):
  field_header_pattern = re.compile(r'\[\[ ## (\w+) ## \]\]')
  Regex handles markers anywhere in line, extracts field name dynamically

**Context:** regex crate already in Cargo.toml (regex = '1.11.2')

**Fix:** Implement regex-based parsing in parse_response() matching DSPy's pattern.

**Refs:**
- DSRs: crates/dspy-rs/src/adapter/chat.rs:582-623 (parse_response)
- DSPy: dspy/adapters/chat_adapter.py:21,212-247 (field_header_pattern, parse)
title: ChatAdapter: Switch to regex for field parsing
